title: Discuss politeness across cultures
subtitle: Different expectations.
overview: Module: Cultural, Social, and Personal Topics. B2 Med strengthens abstract discussion, source response, professional communication, precise style, revision, and independent output.

sections.title: B2 med focus
rows: Politeness is cultural, so the same sentence can feel different in different contexts.
礼貌是文化性的，所以同一句话在不同语境中感觉可能不同。
B2 Med frame: explain the context, avoid stereotypes, add perspective, and connect it to real life.
B2 Med 框架：解释背景、避免刻板印象、加入视角，并连接真实生活。
In some contexts, direct feedback feels honest and helpful. In others, it may feel too sharp unless it is softened with appreciation or explanation.
在一些语境中，直接反馈显得诚实且有帮助。在另一些语境中，如果没有感谢或解释来柔化，它可能显得太尖锐。

items.en: Politeness is cultural, so the same sentence can feel different in different contexts.
items.zh: 礼貌是文化性的，所以同一句话在不同语境中感觉可能不同。

items.en: B2 Med frame: explain the context, avoid stereotypes, add perspective, and connect it to real life.
items.zh: B2 Med 框架：解释背景、避免刻板印象、加入视角，并连接真实生活。

items.en: In some contexts, direct feedback feels honest and helpful. In others, it may feel too sharp unless it is softened with appreciation or explanation.
items.zh: 在一些语境中，直接反馈显得诚实且有帮助。在另一些语境中，如果没有感谢或解释来柔化，它可能显得太尖锐。

items.en: I will make the idea precise before I try to make it sound advanced.
items.zh: 我会先让意思准确，再尝试让它听起来高级。

items.en: A strong B2 answer usually includes a condition, a limitation, or a fair counterpoint.
items.zh: 强 B2 回答通常包括条件、限制或公平的反方观点。

items.en: If the wording feels too abstract, I will add one concrete example from real life.
items.zh: 如果措辞感觉太抽象，我会加入一个来自真实生活的具体例子。

items.en: I can revise my answer for logic, tone, and rhythm.
items.zh: 我可以从逻辑、语气和节奏上修改我的回答。

items.en: This lesson is complete only when I can use the idea in my own words.
items.zh: 只有当我能用自己的话使用这个想法时，这节课才算完成。

lines.speaker: B
lines.en: In some contexts, direct feedback feels honest and helpful. In others, it may feel too sharp unless it is softened with appreciation or explanation.
lines.zh: 在一些语境中，直接反馈显得诚实且有帮助。在另一些语境中，如果没有感谢或解释来柔化，它可能显得太尖锐。

lines.speaker: A
lines.en: Can you make the answer more precise or more concrete?
lines.zh: 你能让回答更准确或更具体吗？

lines.speaker: B
lines.en: Yes. I will narrow the claim, add one example, and mention one limitation.
lines.zh: 可以。我会缩小主张、加入一个例子，并提到一个限制。

items.en: Answer once in six sentences with a clear structure.
items.zh: 用六句话做一次结构清楚的回答。

items.en: Add one B2 phrase such as that said, in practice, arguably, or the real issue is.
items.zh: 加入一个 B2 短语，比如 that said、in practice、arguably 或 the real issue is。

items.en: Record the answer, then rewrite the strongest sentence more naturally.
items.zh: 录下回答，然后把最强的一句改得更自然。

items.en: Use the revised sentence tomorrow in a different topic.
items.zh: 明天在不同话题中使用修改后的句子。
